title: Sanji Says Goodbye to the Baratie
scene: On pages 1–4, the Baratie conflict focuses on Luffy, Sanji, Don Krieg, Gin, Zeff. Sanji leaves the restaurant behind, joining Luffy’s crew while the cooks hide their emotions behind insults.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: What's the meaning of this? Didn't you want a cook for your ship!? What, are you not satisfied with him?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: No, it's not like I'm dissatisfied or anything. I do want him to come Arlong with me,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But he said he wants to stay here and be a cook.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: So even if you tell me to take him with me, I'm not gonna.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So you won't accept unless he says it himself.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: But the problem is whether that rebelliious brat will be honest with himself or not...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: It's impossible! He's too hard headed for his own good.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Daba DBABA BABA BABA BABA BABA BABA BABA BA BA BA BA BA BA BABA BA!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Did he come all the way from fishman Island to try our cooking?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You idiot, this is just a guy in a pandashark.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What're you doing here alone? Where are the others? And what about Nami?

summary: This part keeps the Baratie battle centered on willpower, debt, hunger, and pride. The dialogue shows who still stands firm after Krieg’s violence and who is ready to break away from the restaurant’s old life.
